meaning: n.轫手,火车上控制煞车的煞车手
examples: Later he was a brakeman, clerk, and machinistS helper.
The hearse was manned by a driver and a brakeman - both dressed in black and wearing top hats. The horses were white Percheron with black plumes on their heads.
Langen, and his 37-year-old brakeman Zimmerman, won the right to go last after tossing a coin.
Defending Olympic champion Pierre Lueders of Canada, with his brakeman Giulio Zardo, came home behind the Americans.
The brakeman fell down outside the cable car as he was pushing it and couldn't get back aboard.
